summary: Large gm_16F1 decomp/matching PR that implemented multiple previously undecompiled result/decision helper functions, updated their public prototypes, refined related gm/pl struct layouts, and adjusted one gm_16AE call site after fn_80171DC4 became void(void). The only loaded discussion is an automated report showing 5 new matches, several partial-score improvements in gm_16F1, and one unrelated-looking gmresult regression.

key_files.role: Primary decompilation target; replaces placeholder comments with implementations and improves existing helpers in gm_16F1.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: gm_16F1.c +1192/-42, 8 hunks
raw_diff: fn_8016F39C, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, fn_8016FAD4, fn_8016FFD4, fn_801701C0, fn_80171BA4, fn_80171DC4 implementations
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists gm_16F1 new matches and improvements

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for recovered gm_16F1 signatures.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: gm_16F1.h +9/-9
raw_diff: UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS replaced for fn_8016F39C, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, fn_80171BA4; fn_80171DC4 changed to void(void)

key_files.role: Adds local static data declarations/struct used by the new gm_16F1 implementations.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: gm_16F1.static.h +30/-0
raw_diff: adds lbl_804DA2E8/lbl_804DA2F0 declarations and struct lbl_803B7A60_t with static initializer

key_files.role: Refines game-mode result/rules struct layout used by gm_16F1 code.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: gm/types.h +7/-2
raw_diff: adds x7, xA, x40, and padding changes in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t nested layout

key_files.role: Refines player struct layout with fields used by recovered player/result logic.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: pl/types.h +3/-1
raw_diff: splits x138 padding and adds x13C/x140 unsigned int fields
changed_files.jsonl: gmregclear.c +5/-2
raw_diff: introduces output local for fn_8016F180(i) result before mask check
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report says gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 reached 100%

key_files.role: Call-site update for changed fn_80171DC4 signature.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: gm_16AE.c +1/-1
raw_diff: fn_80171DC4(gobj) changed to fn_80171DC4()

what_changed: Implemented several gm_16F1 functions that were placeholders, including decision/result scanning helpers, scoring/ranking helpers, and result aggregation helpers.
Updated gm_16F1 prototypes from unknown or stale signatures to typed signatures, including fn_8016F1F0 returning s32 and fn_80171DC4 taking no parameters.
Added includes for gm_16AE, plbonuslib, pl/types, and player APIs needed by recovered gm_16F1 code.
Replaced some duplicated table-walk/goto flag extraction in existing gm_16F1 code with calls to fn_8016F180.
Recovered or exposed struct fields in gm/types.h and pl/types.h to support typed field accesses in the new code.
Added local static data declarations and a zero-table struct in gm_16F1.static.h used by the new implementations.
Adjusted gmregclear fn_8017F1B8 with an intermediate output local for the fn_8016F180 result.

Automated report recorded new 100% matches for gm_16F1 fn_80171DC4, fn_8016F548, fn_8016F740, fn_8016F9A8, and gmregclear fn_8017F1B8; it also recorded partial improvements for fn_801701C0, fn_8016FAD4, fn_80171BA4, fn_8016F39C, fn_8016FFD4, and .sdata2.
smart_moves: Header signatures were updated alongside recovered bodies instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in gm_16F1.h.
The gm_16AE call site was kept consistent with the recovered fn_80171DC4 void(void) signature.
Struct layout refinements in gm/types.h and pl/types.h allowed code to use fields such as rules->x58, rules->x7, x58[i].xA, x58[i].x40, and pl x13C/x140 rather than only raw byte offsets.
Repeated decision flag lookup patterns were centralized through fn_8016F180 in multiple gm_16F1 paths.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a signature, update the owning header and direct callers in the same PR slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80171DC4 prototype changed from void fn_80171DC4(HSD_GObj*) to void fn_80171DC4(void)
raw_diff: gm_16AE call changed from fn_80171DC4(gobj) to fn_80171DC4()

decomp_lessons.lesson: Recovering local struct fields can make large result/stat functions reviewable by replacing anonymous padding with typed accesses, but the semantic names here remain mostly address/offset-style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm/types.h adds x7, xA, x40 and padding refinements
raw_diff: pl/types.h adds x13C and x140
raw_diff: gm_16F1.c uses rules->x58, rules->xC, x58[i].xA, x58[i].x20, x58[i].x40

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated table flag lookups, a small recovered helper can simplify generated-looking curr/goto blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8016F870 removes curr table-walk/goto blocks and uses fn_8016F180(i)
raw_diff: fn_80170110 replaces table-walk/goto flag extraction with fn_8016F180(kind)

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style function names were retained for unrecovered semantics while local role names were used where clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: new implementations keep fn_ address-style globals and use descriptive locals in several blocks

naming_conventions.convention: Partially understood struct members keep offset-style names rather than speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm/types.h and pl/types.h field additions use offset-style names
raw_diff: gm_16F1.c accesses x58 and offset-style fields throughout scoring/ranking logic

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow PAD_STACK use after returns in several recovered functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(16) in fn_8016F39C
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(24) in fn_8016F548
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(8) in fn_8016F740/fn_8016F9A8/fn_8016FFD4
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(4) in fn_801701C0
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The automated report supports some matches/improvements, but the loaded evidence does not isolate which PAD_STACK uses were required.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use exact integer widths and casts around masks, indices, and signed stat fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_8016F1F0 return type changed short to s32
raw_diff: repeated casts such as (u8) mask, (u32) i < 0x101U, (s16) lbl_803D5A4C[i].kind, and (s8) rules->xC
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Supported as an observed tactic, not as a proven general requirement.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use switch-based reconstruction for the large bonus/condition dispatcher in fn_801701C0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801701C0 implemented with switch(arg2) cases 0xD7 and onward
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The bot report shows fn_801701C0 improved to 91.77%, not a full match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduce temporary pointer variables for arrays and structs to shape loops over player/result slots.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801701C0 uses x58_p, sc_p, rk_p, q, sq pointer walks
raw_diff: fn_80171BA4 uses player/team loops and Player_* accessors
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Observed in diff; no human review explains intent.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover signatures in headers and update call sites with the body
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2411, recovered gm_16F1 bodies were accompanied by gm_16F1.h prototype updates and a gm_16AE call-site adjustment for fn_80171DC4(void). This is a concrete instance of header truthfulness during decomp matching.

raw_diff: gm_16AE fn_80171DC4 call-site change

accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2411 shows repeated gm_16F1 table scans/goto flag extraction being replaced with fn_8016F180 calls in existing and new functions, improving readability while preserving matching progress in nearby functions.

Future gm_16F1 work may need coordinated gm/types.h and pl/types.h struct layout updates.

rejection_notes: Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
Do not promote fn_801701C0 as matched; bot report says it improved to 91.77%, not 100%.
Do not treat the gmresult fn_80174468 regression as explained or accepted; loaded evidence only reports it.
Do not generalize semantic meanings for offset-style fields x7, xA, x20, x40, x13C, or x140 beyond their observed use in this diff.
